title: Pat Hogan: ‘I’m not going anywhere’ despite overwhelming Syracuse mayoral primary loss
summary: Pat Hogan, a seven-term Syracuse Common Councilor, lost the Democratic mayoral primary on June 27, 2025, receiving 23% of the vote compared to winner Sharon Owens's 62%. Despite the decisive loss—worse than his 2013 primary defeat—Hogan stated he is remaining active in city politics and will continue advocating for community needs.

political_signal.description: Hogan's campaign positioned him in opposition to incumbent Mayor Ben Walsh's administration policies; loss signals voter preference for Owens's platform despite Hogan's official party endorsement and diverse coalition support.

processing_notes: Article is electoral reporting with limited direct CIO relevance unless tracking mayoral transition planning implications
